title: Chinese makers of DRAM modules, SSDs have a serious advantage over American and Taiwanese suppliers, says SMI SVP state guidance secures local DRAM and SSD supply while the Big Three chase AI margins
summary: Chinese DRAM and SSD producers may gain an advantage due to CCP directives. These directives can secure local supply and support the industry. This may allow them to compete with American and Taiwanese suppliers.
insight: CCP directives secure local DRAM and SSD supply advantage

title: Digital entrepreneur creates humorous 'physical NFT minting device' using a Raspberry Pi in quest for 'infinite money machine' contraption trained on M3 MacBook can generate an NFT in 3 seconds, has so far sold one for $9.92
summary: A young entrepreneur creates a portable NFT minting device using a Raspberry Pi, aiming to achieve wealth similar to Elon Musk.
insight: Decentralized and automated NFT creation can potentially disrupt art and collectible markets.

title: Kaspersky finds malware hidden in Steam Wallpaper Engine that hijacks accounts to spread itself dozens of malicious packages downloaded tens of thousands of times
summary: Malware was hidden in Steam Wallpaper Engine, hijacking user accounts to spread itself. Dozens of malicious packages were downloaded tens of thousands of times. Kaspersky discovered the malware after several months of smuggling.
insight: Malicious content can hide in seemingly harmless digital assets

title: Frontier Airlines site leaks all personal info with just a glance at a boarding pass, researcher claims booking number and last name nets you every passenger's personal info, including address, passport, TSA PreCheck, and most credit card info
summary: Frontier Airlines' website exposes passengers' personal information with minimal details, including address, passport, and credit card info, raising concerns over data security and privacy.
insight: Sensitive passenger data vulnerable to unauthorized access via booking number and last name.

title: Intel's fab roadmap examined Arizona, Ohio, Ireland, and the two deadlines deciding 14A process node
summary: Intel's fab roadmap outlines expansions in Arizona, Ohio, Ireland, focusing on 14A process node production capacity. The roadmap sets two key deadlines for achieving this goal, emphasizing the company's ongoing efforts to increase its manufacturing capabilities. This expansion aims to meet growing demand for semiconductors.
insight: Intel invests $100 billion in US to secure future chip supply.

title: US pulls the 'kill-switch' on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI models, sending global allies scrambling European and Canadian leaders alarm allies over sudden export bans
summary: The US blocks Anthropic's Fable 5 AI models, sparking global concern and alarm. World leaders worry about developing national alternatives due to export bans. This move may lead to a fragmented AI landscape.
insight: US AI export controls spark global AI fragmentation fears

title: DeepSeek was set to be added to US Entity List for supporting China s military and intelligence operations, report claims White House holds off to avoid escalating tensions with China
summary: DeepSeek and CXMT are set to be added to the US Entity List due to their alleged ties to Chinese military and intelligence operations. The White House has delayed the move to avoid escalating tensions with Beijing. This decision has significant implications for US-China trade relations.
insight: US avoids escalating tensions with China over trade restrictions

title: Nvidia releases RTX Remix 1.5 with new RTX IO compression reducing mod file sizes by up to 37% update also adds Smooth Normals and 'RTX Remix Skills' Agents
summary: Nvidia's RTX Remix 1.5 update introduces new features including RTX IO compression, which reduces mod file sizes by up to 37%, as well as Smooth Normals and 'RTX Remix Skills' Agents to enhance modding capabilities.
insight: Improved modding efficiency through significant file size reduction efforts.

title: Researchers build brain-like memory device for AI sensors that may improve energy efficiency phototransistor device combines light sensing, memory, and processing to cut data movement
summary: Oregon State University researchers created a brain-inspired phototransistor combining light sensing, memory, and processing. This device can control the persistence of optical memories, potentially improving energy efficiency in AI systems. The innovation aims to reduce data movement and enhance AI vision capabilities.
insight: Optical memory control enhances AI energy efficiency and vision capabilities.

title: Undersea cable connecting Egypt and Syria has been cut, state-owned telecom operator says Damascus blames 'systematic sabotage campaign' as cause of damage
summary: An undersea cable connecting Egypt and Syria has been damaged, prompting accusations of sabotage. The Syrian government blames a 'systematic sabotage campaign' for the damage, without naming a specific entity. The cable's location makes it a prime target for disruption.
insight: High-stakes geopolitics in the Middle East escalate with undersea sabotage

title: First official details of AMD's next-gen 'Mustang Peak' Threadripper CPUs come into view chips feature DDR5, PCIe 6.0, and a new socket
summary: AMD's next-gen 'Mustang Peak' Threadripper CPUs feature DDR5 and PCIe 6.0, with a new socket. The CPUs are based on the Zen 6 architecture, indicating significant performance improvements. This marks a significant upgrade for AMD's high-end desktop lineup.
insight: Enhanced performance and compatibility for high-end desktop applications

title: Broadcom unveils fully-integrated quad-core Wi-Fi 8 access point SoCs, 5G FWA router reference platform
summary: Broadcom has launched three new fully-integrated quad-core Wi-Fi 8 access point SoCs, which offer improved performance and efficiency. The BCM6772 is for mass-market Ethernet routers, the BCM6774 for high-volume Ethernet routers, and the BCM6776 for premium Ethernet tri-band routers. The BCM6776 is also part of a 5G FWA router platform.
insight: Fully-integrated Wi-Fi 8 SoCs enable more efficient and high-performance routers.

title: Rockchip RK3539 quad-core Cortex-A55 SoC shows up in low-cost Android 14 4K TV stick with AV1 codec support
summary: The HS89 T15 TV stick features a Rockchip RK3539 SoC, offering 4K video capabilities and Android 14 support.
insight: Affordable 4K streaming with AV1 codec support becomes a reality.

title: The quest for better chip design accelerates
summary: Synopsys announces Multiphysics Fusion products to improve chip design efficiency, reducing respins and costs.
insight: Optimized chip design saves time and reduces costly redesigns.

title: Best of Sensors: Polar ID aims for better face authentication
summary: Polar ID from Metalenz uses polarization image data to enhance face authentication, offering improved cost and performance compared to conventional methods.
insight: Advanced polarization technology boosts face authentication accuracy and efficiency.

title: Tim Cook confirms Apple price hikes are coming, iPhones could jump $200 or more
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook warns of impending price hikes due to rising memory costs.
insight: Rising memory costs force Apple to consider significant iPhone price jumps.

title: Replace These 11 Default Apps in Windows 11
summary: Windows 11 users can replace default apps with more capable alternatives to enhance their experience. Replacing apps such as Photos, Maps, and Clock can provide improved functionality and features. Users can benefit from more comprehensive apps.
insight: Upgrade Windows 11 with better, more feature-rich default apps

title: Trump says Apple will work with Intel to build chips in the US
summary: Trump announces Apple will collaborate with Intel to build US-based chips.
insight: Boosting US chip manufacturing could strengthen national economic security.

title: Stop Killing Games lost its biggest battle despite 1.3 million signatures, but the fight isn't over
summary: The European Commission rejected a petition with 1.3 million signatures to ban permanent game unplayability.
insight: Gamers' rights and consumer protection are being undermined by industry practices.

title: Firefox has an ambitious new roadmap, the browser is also losing millions of users a month
summary: Mozilla is innovating Firefox with new features, but the browser is losing users. Despite this, the company has published a roadmap outlining upcoming changes. The browser's growth concerns are a major challenge for the company.
insight: Firefox's user decline highlights Mozilla's struggles to compete in the market.

title: AB Download Manager is a free alternative to Internet Download Manager
summary: AB Download Manager is a free, open-source download accelerator and manager. It supports download acceleration, queue management, and pause/resume features. Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
insight: Free alternative to expensive download managers like Internet Download Manager

title: Samsung's foundry is booming as TSMC struggles with demand bags Nvidia, Tesla, and Qualcomm as new clients
summary: Samsung Foundry is gaining new clients, including Nvidia, Tesla, and Qualcomm, as it manufactures advanced semiconductor chips. This shift comes after some companies previously worked exclusively with TSMC. Samsung's foundry is booming due to increased demand.
insight: Samsung gains market share from struggling TSMC demand.

title: Android 17 arrives on Pixel with floating apps, foldable features, and new AI tools
summary: Android 17 brings floating apps, foldable features, and AI tools to Pixel phones, building upon Google's customized version of the operating system.
insight: Context-dependent Android updates reveal the evolving role of Google's services layer.

title: This handheld x86 mini-computer is heading to Kickstarter with swappable accessories and 4G LTE
summary: Mogozen's CG Deck is a handheld x86 mini-computer with swappable accessories and 4G LTE. It offers customization options and compatibility with various operating systems. A crowdfunding campaign is planned on Kickstarter.
insight: Potential game-changer for portable computing with x86 performance and LTE connectivity

title: OpenAI made $13 billion in 2025 and lost $21 billion doing it
summary: OpenAI's revenue surged from $3.7 billion in 2024 to $13.07 billion in 2025, driven by rapid growth in demand for its AI products, particularly ChatGPT, which now has over 900 million weekly users.
insight: Massive revenue growth comes with significant financial risk and volatility.

title: Chrome update fixes 33 security flaws, including seven critical bugs
summary: Google Chrome's latest update (149.0.7827.156) fixes 33 security flaws, including 7 critical bugs.
insight: Vulnerabilities pose significant risk to billions of Chrome users worldwide.

title: The White House app could soon be auto-installed on every DHS work phone
summary: The White House app may be auto-installed on DHS work phones, offering access to official communications and updates.
insight: Monitoring government communications could increase surveillance of DHS employees and contractors.

title: Apple's AI hardware roadmap: camera-equipped AirPods, smart glasses, and foldable iPhones
summary: Apple plans to launch camera-equipped AirPods, smart glasses, and foldable iPhones. The AirPods with cameras, code-named B798, is now slated for a 2027 release. The product lineup marks a significant shift in Apple's AI hardware strategy.
insight: Apple's AI hardware roadmap expands beyond smartphones.

title: Microsoft refreshes Surface Pro and Surface Laptop with Snapdragon X2 chips, longer battery life, and higher prices
summary: Microsoft has updated the Surface Pro and Surface Laptop with new features, including Snapdragon X2 chips and longer battery life, but also higher prices.
insight: Microsoft prioritizes performance and battery life over affordability with new Surface updates.

title: "The retail SSD market has almost disappeared," Silicon Motion says, as OEMs take what's left
summary: The retail SSD market has dwindled due to NAND shortages. Silicon Motion's SSD controllers, previously sold in retail drives, now primarily go to OEMs. This shift affects the availability of SSDs for direct-to-consumer sales.
insight: NAND shortages devastate retail SSD market, favoring OEM sales instead
